description: Digital-arrest scam stage taxonomy and red-flag corpus for FraudShield. Used in two ways: (1) as the knowledge base indexed into ChromaDB for retrieval, and (2) as the reference the LLM classifies a transcript against. Stage = how far the scam has progressed (drives lead-time). Red flags = atomic signals the classifier detects.

stages.name: Setup / Pre-contact
stages.intent: Establish a channel and a plausible-looking origin before the victim engages.
indicators: Incoming call or video call over WhatsApp / Skype / Telegram from an unknown number
Spoofed or international-looking number presented as an official agency line
Caller insists on switching to video immediately
example_lines: Hello, am I speaking with [name]? This is an official call, please switch on your video.
This call is being recorded for an official investigation.

stages.name: Pretext
stages.intent: Plant a false premise that links the victim to a crime or official matter.
indicators: Claims a parcel / SIM / Aadhaar / bank account in the victim's name is linked to a crime
Impersonation of CBI, ED, Customs, TRAI, Narcotics, Cyber Cell, or local police
Reference to a fake case/FIR/complaint number to sound legitimate
example_lines: A parcel sent in your name has been seized at the airport. It contained illegal items.
Your Aadhaar number has been used to open an account involved in money laundering.
This is Inspector [name] from the CBI Cyber Crime division, badge number...

stages.name: Fear & Authority
stages.intent: Overwhelm judgement with fear of arrest and the weight of authority.
indicators: Accusation of money laundering, drug trafficking, or terror financing
Explicit threat of arrest, non-bailable warrant, or account freeze
Appearance in uniform; showing fake ID cards, court documents, or arrest warrants
Fake police-station / courtroom / control-room backdrop on video
example_lines: Your name is in a money-laundering case. A non-bailable warrant has been issued.
If you disconnect this call, you will be arrested within two hours.
I am sending you the arrest warrant now. Read your name on it.

stages.name: Isolation & Control
stages.intent: Cut the victim off from anyone who could break the spell, and assert continuous custody.
indicators: Demand to stay on a continuous video call ('digital custody' / 'digital arrest')
Instruction to tell no one — family, bank, or friends
Framing as a confidential national-security or RBI/Supreme-Court-monitored matter
Instruction not to leave the room, not to cut the call, to stay awake/available
example_lines: You are now under digital arrest. Do not disconnect this video call for any reason.
This matter is strictly confidential. If you tell your family you will be charged with obstruction.
Keep your camera on at all times. We are monitoring you.

stages.name: Extraction
stages.intent: Convert fear into an irreversible financial action.
indicators: Demand to transfer money to a 'safe' / 'RBI verification' / 'escrow' account
Request for OTP, card number, CVV, UPI PIN, or net-banking credentials
Instruction to install a remote-access app (AnyDesk, TeamViewer, QuickSupport)
Framing the transfer as refundable 'verification' that will be returned after the probe
example_lines: To prove the money is clean, transfer it to this RBI-verified account. It will be refunded.
Share the OTP you just received so we can verify your account.
Download AnyDesk so our officer can check your banking activity directly.

stages.name: Prolongation
stages.intent: Extend custody and extract repeatedly until the victim is exhausted or out of funds.
indicators: Further transfer demands after the first payment
New fabricated charges requiring 'additional verification'
Pressure to take loans, break FDs, or borrow to pay
example_lines: The verification found more suspicious funds. You must transfer the remaining balance too.
Your case is not closed. One more payment is required to clear your name today.

red_flags.label: Authority impersonation
red_flags.description: Caller claims to be a government / law-enforcement / regulatory body (CBI, ED, Customs, TRAI, police, RBI).

red_flags.label: Criminal accusation
red_flags.description: Victim is accused of a serious crime (laundering, trafficking, terror financing).

red_flags.label: Arrest / legal threat
red_flags.description: Threat of arrest, warrant, account freeze, or prosecution to induce fear.

red_flags.label: Isolation / secrecy demand
red_flags.description: Instruction to tell no one, stay on the call, or not contact family/bank.

red_flags.label: Continuous-video custody
red_flags.description: Demand to remain on video as 'digital arrest' / 'digital custody'.

red_flags.label: Remote-access app request
red_flags.description: Asked to install AnyDesk / TeamViewer / QuickSupport or similar.

red_flags.label: Credential request
red_flags.description: Asked for OTP, card number, CVV, UPI PIN, or net-banking login.

red_flags.label: Fund-transfer demand
red_flags.description: Asked to transfer money to a 'safe' / 'verification' / 'RBI' account.

red_flags.label: Refundable-verification framing
red_flags.description: Transfer framed as temporary, refundable, or for verification only.

red_flags.label: Platform mismatch
red_flags.description: Official 'arrest/investigation' conducted over WhatsApp/Skype/Telegram video — which no real Indian agency does.

red_flags.label: Urgency / time pressure
red_flags.description: Artificial deadline ('within 2 hours', 'right now') to prevent reflection.

red_flags.label: Fake case/badge reference
red_flags.description: Cites a fabricated FIR/case number, badge number, or official document to appear legitimate.

scoring_guidance.note: Used by L4 fusion. These are starting heuristics to calibrate against your labelled set, not final thresholds.
scoring_guidance.high_risk_condition: Stage >= 3 AND at least one irreversible_action_trigger present, OR three or more 'high'/'critical' red flags co-occurring.
scoring_guidance.caution_condition: Stage 1-2 with one or more 'high' red flags, but no irreversible_action_trigger yet.
scoring_guidance.abstain_condition: Only 'medium' red flags, or ambiguous single signal — stay silent rather than risk a false positive.
scoring_guidance.lead_time_principle: The goal is to reach HIGH-RISK at Stage 2-3, BEFORE Stage 4 extraction. Report the gap between the stage at first HIGH-RISK and the extraction stage as the lead-time metric.

benign_contrast_examples.note: Real calls that share surface features but are NOT scams — use these as hard negatives to drive the false-positive rate down.
examples: A real bank fraud-prevention line asking you to verify a suspicious transaction — but never asking for OTP or a transfer, and willing to let you call back on the official number.
A genuine courier asking you to confirm a delivery address with no threat and no payment demand.
A police verification call for passport/tenant checks that never asks for money and never demands you stay on video.
